title: Dual-Context: B2B Discovery Prep vs Creator Nurture Agent

questions.question: What is the Universal Prep Agent Pattern (5 steps)?

options.text: Gather context, Identify objective, Generate talking points, Prepare questions, Anticipate objections
Profile view, Connection request, Message, Follow-up, Call
ICP match, Score, Enrich, Personalize, Sequence

questions.explanation: The Universal Prep Agent Pattern has five steps that apply to both B2B and creator contexts: gather context (CRM + fresh data), identify objective (what you want from the conversation), generate talking points (tied to their situation), prepare questions (discovery or deepening), and anticipate objections (with responses ready).

questions.question: What is the key difference between B2B Discovery Prep and Creator Nurture Agent input sources?

options.text: B2B uses more tools while creator uses fewer
B2B needs company data, LinkedIn, and email threads; Creator needs content engagement history, email open/click data, and stated goals from opt-in
Creator agents don't need any input data

questions.explanation: B2B Discovery Prep needs CRM research briefs, calendar events, email threads, fresh LinkedIn activity, and company news. Creator Nurture needs CRM contact records, content engagement history (which articles/videos consumed), email open/click data, and stated goals. Same architecture, different data inputs.

questions.question: Why is mastering the dual-context pattern valuable beyond just B2B and creator use cases?

options.text: The real skill is adapting the same agent pattern to different contexts — once mastered, you can build prep agents for any conversation type
It's required by LinkedIn's terms of service
It only works for these two specific contexts

questions.explanation: The lesson teaches that B2B and creator businesses have opposite data structures, yet the prep agent architecture is identical. Mastering this pattern means you can adapt it to any context — investor meetings, partnership calls, customer success check-ins — because the five-step pattern is universal.

questions.question: The B2B Discovery Prep Agent and Creator Nurture Agent use completely different architectures.
questions.explanation: False. Both agents use the same 5-step Universal Prep Agent Pattern. The only difference is what data you feed them and how you frame the output. B2B inputs include company research and email threads; Creator inputs include content engagement and audience data. Same architecture, different context windows.

questions.question: Choose either the B2B or Creator context and describe how you would configure a prep agent for your next scheduled conversation. What data sources would you connect, what output format would you request, and what questions would you want the agent to prepare?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response chooses a specific context (B2B or Creator), identifies relevant data sources for that context, describes a useful output format, and includes conversation-appropriate questions. Look for understanding that the prep agent should reduce cognitive load before calls, not add complexity.
